notes: warnUnexplainedChanges writes a single-line 'warning:' to stderr, never to stdout, and never changes the exit code (documented). Count is deterministic; keep-listed/task-file changes are excluded. No JSON contract impact.

Emits output.JSON(task) on stdout; output.Success is suppressed in JSON mode and otherwise goes to stderr, so no stdout pollution. hc rejection uses exit 2 (ExitUsage) with actionable hcCommitHint; other paths use exit 3/4/1 correctly. Deterministic.

Test correctly asserts the contract: hc rejects tp commit/--auto-commit/bare done with exit 2 and 'commit_strategy is hc' message; a bad batch row is a failed row (partial exit 1, not exit 4); closed/failed counts and failures[].error verified.

Harness always passes --json and unmarshals stdout, confirming JSON stays clean; asserts status/closed_at/closed_reason keys and numeric counts. TP_HC=0 makes auto strategy deterministic. No contract issue.

Output map {id, sha, message} matches the documented Output: {id, sha, message}; message trimmed to first line (no token bloat). hc rejection exit 2 + hcCommitHint (44-48); exit codes 3/4 correct; SetCommitSHAs records single element.

Verifies commit_shas array + commit_sha mirror (commit_shas[0]), duplicate-sha exit 1 with task unchanged, covered-by records none (both keys omitted/nil), reopen clears them, and builtin tp commit records a single-element array. Contract asserted correctly.

warnCommitStrategy emits unrecognized-value and hc-absent warnings to stderr only; neither changes the exit code (documented). Returns the effective builtin/hc name deterministically via engine.ResolveCommitStrategy/EffectiveCommitStrategy. No stdout/JSON impact.

JSON shapes correct: default {workflow, commit_strategy_effective}; --resolved annotates each field with {value, source}. commit_strategy resolves to normalized name. checks serializes as [] (DefaultWorkflow initializes []Check{}), not null. Map keys sorted by json.Marshal (deterministic). --compact no-op documented and consistent with convention.

Verifies --resolved provenance contract: a clamped out-of-range override is attributed to the project/default layer (not 'override') with the resolved value; an unrecognized commit_strategy reports source 'override' and value 'builtin'. Contract asserted correctly.

Single {closed, remaining, has_next, report?}, multi {closed:[], failed:[], remaining, has_next} with make(...,0) so empties are [] not null (382-383, 526-537), batch {closed,failed,skipped,reordered,remaining, failures?} omits failures when empty. Exit codes: multi all-fail 1; batch all-fail 4, partial 1. hc gating exit 2 + hcCommitHint; closure error emits {error,code,acceptance,hint} per doc. Ordered/stable-sorted output is deterministic.

unexplainedChangeCount drops two errors: ClassifyPaths err (L21, degrades to raw list — deliberate) and NormalizeKeepPath err via `skip, _ :=` (L27). NormalizeKeepPath returns (string,error); on failure skip="" so the just-written task file is not excluded and a spurious unexplained-change warning can fire. Warning-only path (§8.3), no exit-code/data impact; low risk but an unjustified ignored return.

WriteTaskFile (L169) is inside WithFileLock (L128). Gate runs pre-flock, then re-read + re-validate ValidTransition and VerifyClosure inside the lock guard TOCTOU. All errors checked and surfaced via output.Error/os.Exit; no swallowed errors, no lock-free write path.

Test file: t.TempDir auto-cleanup, os.WriteFile 0o600, filepath.Join for all paths, require.NoError on every setup step. Bare type assertions (e.g. L91 failures.([]any)) only fail the test on mismatch — no production safety surface.

TestMain builds to os.MkdirTemp and RemoveAll on teardown (panics on build error). runTP handles exec.ExitError via comma-ok (L56) and t.Fatalf on any other error (L58-59). os.RemoveAll error at L32 ignored — conventional harmless test cleanup. 0o600 writes, filepath.Join paths.

`_ = runGit("reset","HEAD","--","*.lock","*.tasks.json.lock")` (L105) swallows the unstage error. In a HEAD-less repo (no commits yet) `git reset HEAD` fails; the flock file staged by `git add -A` (gitStage default, L170) stays staged and the first tp commit commits it. WriteTaskFile is correctly inside WithFileLock (L50/L128); this is the only gap.

Test file: setupCommitProject in a temp git repo, os.WriteFile 0o600, filepath.Join, require.NoError on all setup and json.Unmarshal. Type assertions (res["sha"].(string)) only fail the test on mismatch. No production safety concern.

resolveEffectiveStrategy drops the LoadTaskWorkflowOverride error via `taskOverride, _ :=` (L19); it returns (WorkflowOverride,error) on file-read/JSON failure. A malformed task file's commit_strategy is silently treated as unset so the hc gate may not fire, but every caller (done/close/commit) re-reads the task file downstream and aborts with exit 3, so no unsafe state. Unlike config.go's documented drop, this one is unjustified.

surfaceConfigWarnings deliberately drops LoadProjectConfig/LoadLocalConfig errors (L93-94) — documented (L84-87) and genuinely redundant: resolveConfigWorkflow handles MalformedConfigError via errors.As -> exit 3 with a repair hint (L110-118). No task-file write here; DiscoverTaskFile err-branch is an intentional project-layer-only fallback. Intentional and safe.

Test file: t.TempDir, os.Mkdir 0o755 for dirs, os.WriteFile 0o600 for config/task files, filepath.Join, require.NoError on all writes, t.Chdir for isolation. Type assertions only fail the test on mismatch. No production safety surface.

All three WriteTaskFile calls (L279 single, L495 multi, L769 batch) are inside WithFileLock (L169/L374/L611). Multi snapshots pre-gate status and re-checks inside the lock to abort atomically on concurrent state change (L360-401); TOCTOU guarded. resolveCommitSHAs (L874-893) trims/dedups and returns a propagated error on duplicate SHAs. readBatchEntries uses defer f.Close and %w-wrapped errors. JSON slices use make([]T,0) so no nil->null (closedIDs/failed L382-383; failures gated by len). One benign `data,_ := json.Marshal(errOut)` (L239) marshals only strings+int and cannot fail.
New file; both helpers have doc comments referencing 8.3, clear names, best-effort error handling appropriate for a warning path, no nil-slice-to-JSON hazard, no dead code/TODO.

hc-strategy guard uses shared resolveEffectiveStrategy/hcCommitHint/engine.CommitStrategyHC; warnUnexplainedChanges added post-write; follows output.Error+os.Exit and DiscoverTaskFile conventions. runClose length reflects the pre-existing pre-flock/re-flock pattern, not this change.
New test file; documented setupCloseStrategyProject helper, descriptive test names, consistent assert/require usage matching the package's existing test style.
TP_HC=0 env addition to runTP is well-commented explaining the determinism rationale (5.2); rest of the shared test harness is unchanged and conformant.

hc guard uses shared helper+constant; SetCommitSHAs mirror recorded correctly; helpers well-named. Implicit-claim block duplicates done.go but that pattern predates v0.28.0. runCommit ~105 lines within cobra-handler tolerance.
Clear taskState helper, descriptive test names covering mirror/single/duplicate/covered-by/reopen/builtin cases, consistent with existing cli_test conventions.

New file; hcCommitHint constant and both functions documented with spec anchors; warns via stderr as a side effect without touching exit code. Minor: warnCommitStrategy names return 'effective' but returns local 'eff' (cosmetic, not a defect). No dead code.

commit_strategy_effective and resolved commit_strategy source wired via shared helpers; sourceLabel/resolvedConfig/resolveConfigWorkflow documented; output via output.JSON. ProjectWorkflowOverride re-read a few times per invocation but each is a cheap read, not a maintainability defect.
Two focused tests with doc comments citing 3.4/10.7/10.8; assertions match the resolved-source contract; consistent with in-package test style.

hc guard centralized in runDone for single/multi and separately (by design) in runDoneBatch for failed-row semantics; resolveCommitSHAs is a well-documented helper using make([]string,0,...) avoiding the null-vs-[] bug. Large runDone* functions are pre-existing, not introduced here.
tp resume oracle exists: reads durable state (task file, spec, .tp-review/, .tp/local.json, git) and reports phase + next action. engine.AssembleResume backs it.
commit_strategy has real behavior; hc delegation gates commit-making commands (done.go:127-138) while tp keeps the task-commit record via commit_shas.
keep_uncommitted keep-list stored in git-ignored .tp/local.json; durable cross-reset memory of deliberately-uncommitted files with reasons.
tp is resumable and read-only; no command resets or controls agent context (no reset command exists). State reconstructed from disk alone.
A wip task is surfaced in payload.task with wip:true; keep-list adds clarity (kept vs changes) via ClassifyPaths, not enforcement.
Durable note channels: commit_shas (task.go), closed_reason, keep-entry reason (projectconfig.go:38-41); resume reads them. No context-only planning.
Report-not-force: tp warns on unexplained changes but never commits/discards; no exec of hc anywhere (only LookPath probe).
hc is optional and auto-detected via exec.LookPath only; tp never invokes it. Resume/keep/commit-record reuse existing state files.
phase field: enum review/decompose/implement/audit/release defined as constants and set on ResumeResult.Phase.
spec field: resolved spec path string carried on ResumeResult.Spec.
changes: repo-root-relative paths not on keep-list, byte-sorted (sort.Strings); empty for non-git tree (worktree.go:23-26 returns []).
kept: []{path,reason}, first-matching-entry reason (keeplistmatch.go:24-34), byte-sorted on path.
next_action {command,summary,payload}; command is *string, null for decompose/release (170-173).
blockers array in fixed code order; empty slice (make([],0)) when agent may run next_action.
builtin: tp performs the commit; tp commit / done --auto-commit / done --commit all work (commit.go, done.go builtin path).
hc: tp makes no commit; agent records via done --commit; tp commit and done --auto-commit rejected (commit.go:44-48, done.go:128-132).
auto resolves to hc when hc on PATH else builtin (EffectiveCommitStrategy).
Non-goal upheld: no command clears/compacts/spawns context; no reset command exists. resume is read-only.
Non-goal upheld: no driver binary and no runtime-specific integration shipped; reference driver is documentation.
Non-goal upheld: resume reads existing Converged/StateStale; convergence semantics unchanged.
Non-goal upheld: commit_strategy in readOnlyWorkflowFields, rejected by tp set --workflow (294); authored only by tp init / .tp/config.json.
Non-goal upheld: tp neither invokes hc nor plans hunks; only exec.LookPath("hc") probes presence. No exec.Command("hc") anywhere.
Non-goal upheld: SetCommitSHAs records the provided list; tp does not divide a diff or generate SHAs.
Non-goal upheld: under hc tp makes no commit, so authors no message; task-commit link is commit_shas. buildCommitMessage used only in builtin path.
Non-goal upheld: no hard clean-tree gate; warns only, never commits or discards; keep-list records intent.
Non-goal upheld: keep-entry is structured {path,reason}; no free-text hand-off file.
Non-goal upheld: keep-list lives in .tp/local.json, kept git-ignored by EnsureTPGitignore; repo-scoped, not spec-scoped.
Non-goal upheld: matching is filepath.Match; no ** recursive glob.
Non-goal upheld: release is terminal; no persisted done marker.
Boundary 'review converged before decompose' embodied by phase=decompose (zero tasks + review converged); reset-yes is documented driver policy (SKILL.md).
Boundary 'between review rounds' embodied by phase=review (zero tasks, unconverged). Reset-yes is reference-driver policy.
Boundary 'decomposition imported' embodied by phase=implement (tasks exist, not all done). Reset-yes is driver policy.
Boundary 'between implementation tasks' embodied by phase=implement. Reset-yes is driver policy documented in SKILL.md.
Boundary 'all tasks done before audit' embodied by phase=audit (all done, audit unconverged). Reset-yes is driver policy.
Boundary 'between audit rounds' embodied by phase=audit. Reset-yes is reference-driver policy.
Boundary 'audit converged before release' embodied by phase=release with next_action.command=null; reset-no (human release) is driver policy.
unexplained-changes: agent-clearable when len(changes)>0; data.count. Emitted first.
no-ready-task: escalate; implement with open tasks but no ready task; data.blocked_by = unmetDeps.
review-budget-exhausted: escalate; phase review, rounds>=cap, not converged, cap!=0 (0 never fires); data.cap.
audit-budget-exhausted: escalate; phase audit, rounds>=cap!=0, not converged; data.cap.
spec-stale: escalate; ReviewStale set while phase implement/audit; data.spec. StateStale = last-round hash mismatch (reviewstate.go:172-177).

commit_shas []string omitempty (line 34); UnmarshalJSON mirrors commit_sha=commit_shas[0] when non-empty (88-92); pre-0.28.0 commit_sha-only reads back unchanged (commit_shas absent stays nil, commit_sha has no omitempty).

Plain tp config adds top-level commit_strategy_effective (builtin/hc after auto); --resolved keeps commit_strategy {value,source} (57-60); unrecognized + hc-absent print stderr warnings via warnCommitStrategy (commitstrategy.go:29-38).

CLAUDE.md:213 updates self-dev rule for hc close flow; SKILL.md (12 hits), README.md (17 hits), skills/tp/REFERENCE.md (12 hits) document tp resume, tp keep, commit_strategy, and the reference driver.
--commit repeatable (StringArrayVar:50); resolveCommitSHAs records ordered, duplicate exits 1 (105-110); SetCommitSHAs mirrors commit_sha=[0] (274-276); covered-by records neither.

After close, done/close print one-line stderr warning naming unexplained-change count and exit 0 (done.go:285, close.go:175); keep-listed change not counted; task file excluded; tp neither commits nor discards.
tp keep add/update, --remove (absent no-op exit 0), --list prints [] when empty; missing reason (57-61) and malformed glob (63-67) exit 2; path stored repo-root-relative from any subdir.
MatchKeepList: filepath.Match, first matching entry wins + reason; ClassifyPaths splits kept {path,reason} vs unmatched changes; malformed pattern surfaces filepath.ErrBadPattern.
LocalConfig.KeepUncommitted *[]KeepEntry omitempty {path,reason}; absent (nil ptr) marshals omitted, explicit empty (non-nil ptr) marshals [].

UpdateKeepList reads/writes .tp/local.json under WithFileLock; NormalizeKeepPath stores repo-root-relative; LoadLocalConfig (projectconfig.go:214-224) round-trips presence (absent stays absent, empty stays []).
commit_shas in managedFields -> tp set rejects exit 2; reopen.go:58-60 clears commit_sha, commit_shas, gate_passed_at, gate_skipped_reason.
DetectPhase task-first ordering: implement/audit/release/decompose/review; open task -> implement even when review unconverged (stale). Tested in resumephase_test.go.
AssembleResume builds {phase,spec,changes,kept,next_action,blockers}; changes/kept byte-sorted (38-39); no write (read-only).
Blocker {code,class,message,data}; fixed emission order; unexplained-changes agent-clearable, no-ready-task/spec-stale/budget escalate; budget phase-gated, never fires at cap 0.
tp resume: spec argument wins (34-43), exits 3 when no task file (46-51); emits JSON when piped; --compact strips summary/kept reason/blocker message keeping data (86-120); writes no file.
BuildNextAction per phase: review/audit round=recorded+1, unresolved=0 first round; implement previews {task,wip}; decompose/release command null.
Under effective hc: tp commit (commit.go:44-48), done --auto-commit, bare done rejected exit 2 with hint; close.go:94-98 same; batch bad row failed (745-748); never exit 4.
ResolveCommitStrategy: task-over-project precedence via pickString, auto default; auto->hc/builtin by HasHC; present unrecognized->builtin, absent->auto.
Tests {code,class,message,data} shapes; agent-clearable + escalate classes; fixed emission order; budget-exhausted at cap; cap 0 never fires; clean is empty-not-nil.
Tests builtin commit/auto-commit; effective hc rejects commit/auto-commit/bare done exit 2 (not 4); commit + covered-by close; batch bad row failed exit 1; no exit-4 path.
Tests two commits with mirror, single, duplicate exit 1, covered-by records none, set rejects, reopen clears, builtin commit records single-element commit_shas.
Tests --compact strips next_action.summary/kept reason/blocker message keeping data; resume writes no file and emits JSON when piped.
Tests no task file + no arg exits 3; spec argument wins over discovered; absent adjacent task file yields review (decompose branch covered in phase/payload tests).
Tests unexplained change makes done exit 0 with stderr count and stays unmodified; keep-listed change produces no warning.

Tests add/overwrite/remove/list ([] when empty); remove-absent exit 0; missing reason exit 2; malformed glob exit 2; subdir stored repo-root-relative; writes only local.json (no task/.tp-review file).
Tests implement prefers wip / first ready / null+no-ready-task blocker (blocked_by); review+audit round=1 unresolved=0; decompose+release null command and empty payload.
Tests DetectPhase all transitions and AssembleResume: implement-even-when-stale (+spec-stale blocker), audit, release, decompose, review from durable state.
Tests resolution precedence, auto default, effective hc/builtin reporting, present unrecognized value -> builtin with warning.
Tests modified/staged/untracked in changes with unexplained-changes count; staged rename destination; tp keep moves to kept; glob matches two files -> two kept entries; non-git dir empty arrays.
git status --porcelain=v1 -z -uall parsed to repo-root-relative paths; staged rename (R/C) yields destination only (skips origin field); untracked enumerated individually; non-git dir -> empty slice.
